name: search_memories
description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this before responding to retrieve context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions. Do not use this to store memories - use add_memory for that. Do not use this to see all stored memories - use list_memories for that.

query.description: Natural language query text to find relevant memories. Example: "user preferences for updates" or "past project interests"

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return. Must be a positive integer.

user.description: User identifier (user ID or handle) to search memories for. Defaults to configured user if not provided.

name: add_memory
description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when the user explicitly says "remember this" or when storing conversation context for future retrieval. The LLM automatically extracts, deduplicates, and merges related memories. Do not use this to search or retrieve memories - use search_memories for that.

text.description: Simple text memory to store. Use this for single-sentence preferences or facts. Example: "User prefers concise updates". Use instead of messages parameter.

messages.description: Array of conversation message objects with role and content fields to extract memories from. Each object should have {"role": "user" | "assistant", "content": "message text"}. Use instead of text parameter.

user.description: User identifier (user ID or handle) to store memory for. Defaults to configured user if not provided.

name: list_memories
description: Lists all stored memories for a user with their IDs and creation timestamps. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. Do not use this for semantic search - use search_memories for that.

user.description: User identifier (user ID or handle) to list memories for. Defaults to configured user if not provided.

name: delete_memory
description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories by ID, or to clear all memories for a user. Requires memory_id to delete a specific memory, or both all=true and user to delete all memories.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete. Obtain this from the id field in results of search_memories or list_memories. Use instead of the all parameter.

all.description: If true, deletes all memories for the user. Use instead of memory_id parameter. Requires user parameter to be set.

user.description: User identifier (user ID or handle) to delete memories for. Required when using all=true.
